untranslatable_reason: Signal requires tracking a sequence counter of contradictory signals (4th, 7th occurrence) with position sizing rules (reduce by 25% on 4th, exit on 7th). No allowed indicator directly measures 'contradictory signal count' or implements dynamic position-sizing based on signal history. This requires stateful rule logic beyond indicator-based conditions. The confidence_decay_multiplier and signal_lookback_periods parameters also require custom state management not supported by standard indicator operators.
translation_notes: Attempted proxy: Entry when trend established (ADX > 25) and price positioned in direction of trend (price vs SMA_20). Exit when conviction weakens (RSI moving toward exhaustion zones). However, this does not capture the core mechanism: counting contradictory signals against the thesis and scaling out progressively. A true implementation would need a rule engine that maintains a contradiction counter, compares incoming signals against the established thesis, and triggers position reductions at thresholds 4 and 7. The signal is fundamentally about thesis management and confidence decay, not price-based technical indicators.
